message:
fix: io_uring panic→error, blocking pool last-worker handoff, TCP listener timer driver

io_uring (fs/uring.rs):
- ReadFuture, WriteFuture: replace panic on re-poll with io::Error
  via polled_after_completion_error() helper — panicking in poll()
  is unsound in executors that catch panics
- SyncFuture: add missing `done` flag (previously had no re-poll
  guard at all, meaning fsync/fdatasync could be issued twice)
- Read/Write: restructure to only update file position on Ok(n),
  not on error — previously the ? operator would propagate the error
  but the position update preceded it in the code path

Blocking pool (runtime/blocking_pool.rs):
- Worker exit path now checks for stranded work: if the last active
  worker is exiting but the queue still has pending tasks (from a
  spawn that linearized before shutdown), it spawns a replacement
  thread and notifies the condvar before completing its exit
- This closes a race where accepted-but-not-yet-dispatched work
  could be stranded during shutdown
- New test handle_spawn_accepted_before_shutdown_still_runs
  reproduces the race using on_thread_stop hooks to control timing
- Import reordering (rustfmt canonical)

TCP listener (net/tcp/listener.rs):
- from_std() now uses the runtime's timer driver (via Cx::current())
  instead of wall_clock_now(), so accept-storm backoff timing is
  consistent with the runtime's time source (important for virtual
  clock testing and deterministic replay)
- Renamed wall_clock_now → listener_now to reflect the new behavior
- New test accept_storm_window_from_std_uses_runtime_timer_driver
  verifies that a VirtualClock advances the storm window correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
